question.text: An exporter who will receive EUR after 3 months wants to hedge currency risk. What is the appropriate strategy using exchange-traded currency derivatives?

answer.text: Sell EURINR futures or buy EURINR put options
Buy EURINR futures or buy EURINR call options
Sell USDINR futures or buy USDINR put options
Buy EURUSD futures or sell EURUSD put options

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An exporter receiving EUR faces risk of EUR depreciation against INR. Selling EURINR futures locks in the conversion rate. Buying EURINR puts provides insurance against EUR decline while retaining upside. **The Trap:** Buying futures/calls would hedge an importer who needs to pay in EUR, not an exporter who will receive EUR. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Chapter 5 covers currency hedging for exporters/importers. Series XIII compares hedging approaches across asset classes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the rule: Receive foreign currency = Sell futures/Buy puts. Pay foreign currency = Buy futures/Buy calls.
